The schedule lets the caller see at which q the recovery becomes adequate.

doc: Inverse dynamics (Newton-Euler-style for closed-form M, C, g):   τ = M(q) q̈ + C(q, q̇) q̇ + g(q)

name: parseMIMOMatrices
doc: Parse MIMO state-space from textarea inputs.

name: dcGain
doc: Compute DC gain matrix G(0) = -C·A^{-1}·B + D

name: rgaSteady
doc: Compute RGA = G ⊗ (G^{-T}) for square m×m system at steady state.

name: staticDecoupler
doc: Static decoupler: W = G(0)^{-1} (yields G(0)·W = I). Returns m×m static matrix W. Caller can apply via applyDecoupler.

name: dynamicDecouplerAtFrequency
doc: Frequency-specific dynamic decoupler prototype: W(jωc) = G(jωc)^-1, verification = G(jωc)·W(jωc). This is not yet a polynomial matrix dynamic compensator. It is a selected- frequency inverse used to quantify whether dynamic decoupling is plausible around a chosen crossover frequency.

name: applyDecoupler
doc: Apply a static decoupler W (m×m) to a MIMO plant: B' = B·W, D' = D·W. Returns a new MIMOStateSpace with decoupled inputs.

name: rgaDiagnosis
doc: Diagnose pairing quality from RGA diagonal.

name: evalAtJw
doc: Evaluate complex matrix G(jω) = C·(jωI - A)^{-1}·B + D. Returns p×m grid of Complex numbers.

name: singularValues
doc: Singular values of complex matrix G via eigenvalues of G^H · G.

name: dynamicRGA
doc: Dynamic RGA: Λ(jω) = G(jω) .* (G(jω)^{-T})   where Λ_{ij} = G_{ij}(jω) · [G^{-1}(jω)]_{ji}

name: dynamicRGAMagnitude
doc: Like dynamicRGA but returns magnitudes |Λ_{ij}(jω)| as plain numbers.

name: dynamicRGADiagonal
doc: Returns only the diagonal RGA magnitudes [|Λ_{11}|, |Λ_{22}|, …] per frequency. Useful for monitoring coupling as a function of frequency.

name: characteristicLoci
doc: Characteristic loci: eigenvalues λ_i(G(jω)) across frequency. These are used in MIMO Nyquist-style design to see whether loop eigenvalues encircle the critical point after pairing / decoupling.

name: gershgorinBands
doc: Gershgorin bands for G(jω): center = G_ii(jω), radius = Σ_{j≠i}|G_ij(jω)|. A small radius/center ratio indicates weaker loop interaction around ω.

name: inverseNyquistArray
doc: Inverse Nyquist Array (INA): entries of G(jω)^-1 across frequency. Useful when designing diagonal controllers on inverse plant channels.

name: singularValueBode
doc: Compute σ_max(ω), σ_min(ω) across frequency grid.

name: minrealSS
doc: Compute the minimum realisation of a state-space system (A, B, C, D) by removing uncontrollable and unobservable states using SVD-based rank tests on the controllability and observability Gramians (or matrices). doc: Compute Hankel singular values of a stable LTI system (A, B, C, D). The Hankel singular values are the square roots of the eigenvalues of Wc · Wo, where Wc and Wo are the controllability and observability Gramians. Equivalently, they are the singular values of Lc^T · Lo.

name: hankelNorm
doc: Hankel norm of a stable LTI system — the largest Hankel singular value. ‖G‖_H = σ₁(Wc, Wo)

name: hankelNormApprox
doc: Hankel norm approximation of order k for a stable LTI system. Uses balanced truncation (P25-01) for the reduced state matrices, which for balanced realizations achieves the optimal Hankel norm error σ_{k+1} (Glover 1984 / AAK theorem). name: solveSetpointSteadyState
doc: Compute the steady-state input u_ss required for the state x_ss = r. Steady-state condition: r = Ad·r + Bd·u_ss  →  (I−Ad)·r = Bd·u_ss Solution: u_ss = pinv(Bd) · (I−Ad) · r   (minimum-norm if underdetermined) Also returns the residual ‖Bd·u_ss − (I−Ad)·r‖∞ (= 0 when r is exactly reachable).

name: firstMpcActionTracking
doc: One step of receding-horizon setpoint-tracking MPC. The state error e_k = x_k − r and the input increment v_k = u_k − u_ss satisfy the SAME dynamics as (Ad, Bd), so standard MPC is run on e_0 and v returned. The actual control is u = v + u_ss. constraints: same shape as firstMpcActionConstrained ({

name: simulateMpcTracking

name: solveOutputSetpointSteadyState
doc: MIMO output-space setpoint steady-state solver. Finds x_ss, u_ss satisfying:   x_ss = A_d x_ss + B_d u_ss   y_ref = C x_ss + D u_ss Non-square plants use a regularized least-squares solution, so the residual explicitly reports whether the requested output setpoint is exactly reachable.

name: simulateMpcOutputTracking
doc: Receding-horizon MPC tracking for MIMO output references y_ref. reference may be a fixed p×1 matrix or a function (k) → p×1.

name: firstMpcActionConstrained
doc: Solve one constrained MPC step (receding-horizon: return only u_0). constraints:   { uMin: number|number[], uMax: number|number[] }   — per-step box bounds on u   { A: number[][], b: number[] }                     — general polyhedral: A·U_seq ≤ b   Both can be combined.  Per-input arrays or scalars

doc: Simulate constrained MPC in receding-horizon fashion. Returns same shape as simulateUnconstrainedMpc plus activeConstraintsLog.

name: simulateStateConstrainedMpc
doc: Simulate MPC with input and soft state constraints in receding-horizon fashion.
